L1.area: Expression evaluation and runtime property resolution

L2.description: A prototype pollution vulnerability exists in a JavaScript sandboxing library where prototype chain validation during property access is conditionally applied, allowing it to be bypassed for certain object types. An attacker can exploit this gap to obtain references to object prototypes outside the sandbox boundary, which can then be used to construct arbitrary function calls and achieve remote code execution in the host Node.js environment.

L3.area: Expression evaluation and runtime property resolution
L3.description: A prototype pollution vulnerability exists in a JavaScript sandboxing library where prototype chain validation during property access is conditionally applied, allowing it to be bypassed for certain object types. An attacker can exploit this gap to obtain references to object prototypes outside the sandbox boundary, which can then be used to construct arbitrary function calls and achieve remote code execution in the host Node.js environment.

ground_truth.reason: The sandbox's property access handler applies prototype chain checks only in an else branch, allowing function objects to bypass the validation and access dangerous prototype methods like __lookupGetter__, enabling sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.
